title: 'When you read his manifesto, he hates Christians': Trump calls WH gunman 'sick guy'
content: President Trump described the man who attempted to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner as a "sick guy" who "hates Christians," citing a manifesto. The suspect, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, was armed and subdued by Secret Service before entering the venue, preventing a potential attack on attendees including the president.

title: Narine magic and Rinku firepower seal Super Over heist for KKR
content: Kolkata Knight Riders won a thrilling Super Over against Lucknow Super Giants. The match ended tied after regulation time. Sunil Narine was the hero in the Super Over, taking crucial wickets. Rinku Singh also played a vital role with bat and ball. LSG's chase faltered despite good starts. KKR's win lifts them in the standings.

title: Microsoft Foundry update: Each AI agent gets its own cloud computer
content: Microsoft is addressing enterprise software industry fears about AI-driven job cuts with its Foundry platform.  The new Hosted Agents feature provides each AI agent with its own dedicated cloud sandbox, effectively treating them as licensed users. This strategy aims to maintain software license revenue by ensuring companies pay for AI agents, even as human headcount decreases.

title: After backing Trump's child savings plan, Michael Dell's next $750 m going to hospital
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 39: Ranveer Singh film chases 'Pushpa 2’s domestic record
content: On Day 39, Ranveer Singh’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is on the cusp of making history as it chases the worldwide record of SS Rajamouli’s 'Baahubali 2'. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the espionage thriller has reached a global gross of Rs. 1,772.94 crore, needing just Rs. 15.12 crore more to become the second highest-grossing Indian film ever. Domestically, the film is locked in an intense battle to dethrone 'Pushpa 2: The Rule' as the all-time India net champion.

title: 'Iran can call us': Trump rules out travel for talks as Araghchi returns to Pakistan
content: US President Donald Trump declared American officials won't travel for Iran truce talks, insisting discussions must be remote. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi returned to Islamabad, relaying Tehran's positions on nuclear issues and the Strait of Hormuz via Pakistani intermediaries. Iran maintains it won't negotiate under coercion amid ongoing US pressure.

title: Ronaldo’s 3 final career goals before retirement – Messi already ahead
content: Cristiano Ronaldo is entering the final phase of his career with three clear goals still driving him forward: a first World Cup title in 2026, the pursuit of 1,000 career goals, and the rare chance of playing alongside his son at Al Nassr. Even at 41, he remains central for club and country, shaping both legacy and milestones.

title: Dianna Russini remark on husband resurfaces amid Mike Vrabel buzz
content: Dianna Russini is facing renewed scrutiny as an old joke about marrying Kevin Goldschmidt for his “big wallet” resurfaces amid controversy involving Mike Vrabel. The remark, later clarified as humor, has been widely misinterpreted online. At the same time, rumors around leaked photos and her alleged link to Vrabel continue to circulate, though unverified, keeping the debate alive across social media and sports circles.

title: LeBron, Bronny make NBA history with first father-son playoff alley-oop
content: LeBron James and Bronny James made NBA playoff history with the league’s first father-son scoring connection, as LeBron set up an alley-oop finished by Bronny in Game 3 against the Houston Rockets. Beyond the highlight, the moment underscored LeBron’s unmatched longevity, allowing their careers to overlap in a meaningful, competitive playoff setting rather than a symbolic appearance.

title: Hindu temple caretaker in Bangladesh found hanging from tree, probe under way
content: A Hindu temple caretaker in southeastern Bangladesh, Nayan Sadhu, was found dead hanging from a tree three days after he went missing. Police recovered his decomposed body and are investigating whether it was a murder or suicide, with community leaders suspecting foul play due to the circumstances.

title: Vrabel saga: Rumours swirl over paternity of Dianna Russini’s son amid scandal
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has publicly announced that he is taking some time off to seek counselling as the alleged affair rumors with Dianna Russini refuse to die down as new details emerge. Amid all the mess, it seems the situation has taken a turn for the worse as Dianna Russini’s husband and child have been dragged into the drama.

title: How Israel deployed Iron Dome to UAE amid Iran war - Explained
content: In a significant shift, Israel has deployed its advanced Iron Dome air defence system to the UAE amid sustained Iranian attacks. This marks the first operational deployment outside Israel, highlighting deepening regional military ties. The system successfully intercepted numerous projectiles, bolstering UAE defenses and solidifying alliances against common threats.

title: 'Sulking gets you nowhere': Ishan Kishan drops truth bomb on India snub
content: Kishan returned to the India setup earlier this year for the T20I series against New Zealand and the T20 World Cup after strong performances in domestic cricket, where he led Jharkhand to the Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy (SMAT) title and scored more than 500 runs.

title: Cardi B sparks dating rumors as Stefon Diggs custody buzz grows
content: Cardi B sparks fresh dating rumors after a viral video shows her with an Atlanta-based barber, fueling online chatter. The clip arrives amid ongoing speculation about Cardi B and Patriots WR  Stefon Diggs, with unverified claims of a breakup, a DNA controversy, and a custody battle making the rounds. While fans react strongly to the viral moment, no official confirmation has been provided to support the circulating reports. Stefon Diggs also faces renewed attention as past scrutiny resurfaces. The situation highlights how quickly social media narratives around Cardi B and Stefon Diggs can spread without verified facts.

title: Two former Israel PMs unite to challenge Netanyahu in upcoming elections
content: Opposition leader Yair Lapid and former Prime Minister Naftali Bennett are joining forces for upcoming parliamentary elections, aiming to unseat Benjamin Netanyahu. This unification of their parties signals a significant political realignment in Israel. Both leaders have been critical of Netanyahu's governance, particularly his handling of recent conflicts. Their previous coalition briefly ended Netanyahu's long tenure.

title: MS Dhoni's calf injury worsened after warm-up game, reveals CSK coach
content: Fleming said the injury remains a concern because another setback could rule Dhoni out for the whole season.

title: Worst in the league: Rahane-Seifert fail again as KKR's opening woes continue
content: Tim Seifert was the first to go in the final ball of the second over. Mohsin Khan bowled a full delivery around off stump, and Seifert chipped it straight to Mukul Choudhary at covers. He was dismissed for 0 off 3 balls, giving Mohsin a wicket-maiden over.

title: 'Gaddar Raghav Chadha': AAP workers detained while protesting outside MP's house - video
content: Mumbai Police detained several Aam Aadmi Party activists protesting outside Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha's residence after he joined the BJP. Party workers raised slogans, burned his effigy, and held placards expressing anger over his switch. This follows similar demonstrations in Ludhiana against MP Rajinder Gupta.

title: Gold and silver outlook: Where are precious metals headed this week? Analysts answer
content: Precious metals gold and silver are poised for continued pressure this week, influenced by US-Iran peace talks, central bank decisions, and key economic data. Analysts anticipate volatility as investors weigh geopolitical shifts, inflation concerns, and a strong US dollar.  Domestic gold and silver futures saw significant drops, reflecting global market trends.

title: 'Trump and ... ': US official reveals who was gunman's target at White House shooting
content: A lone gunman, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, disrupted the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, exchanging fire with law enforcement. Investigators suspect he may have targeted Trump administration members. Allen traveled from California and was found with multiple weapons. President Trump was safely evacuated during the incident.

title: 'Dil' of India turns choking lungs: Can Delhi fix its pollution crisis?
content: The air quality crisis in Delhi is an ongoing battle, often leading to emergency protocols like the GRAP being activated as pollution levels soar. While these short-term fixes bring momentary relief, they overlook the fundamental problems. To forge a path toward long-lasting improvements, we must commit to a year-round strategy to curb emissions from vehicles, factories, and farming activities.

title: Angkrish Raghuvanshi’s ‘obstructing the field’ dismissal sparks drama in Lucknow
content: Angkrish Raghuvanshi's controversial 'obstructing the field' dismissal marked a pivotal moment in KKR's IPL 2026 struggles against LSG.  The unusual exit, only the fourth in IPL history, added to Kolkata's woes after a shaky start.  The decision sparked visible frustration from Raghuvanshi and the KKR dugout, intensifying the pressure on both struggling teams.

title: Himanta wife passport row: Khera moves SC for anticipatory bail after setback from HC
content: The Gauhati high court had on April 24 rejected Khera's plea for anticipatory bail in the case registered against him by the Crime Branch of Assam Police in Guwahati. The case against Khera was registered after he claimed at a press brief that ​​Assam CM's wife Riniki Bhuyan "holds multiple foreign passports and undisclosed assets abroad".

title: Are Trump, Netanyahu in sync? How US and Israel are fighting different wars on same battlefield
content: As a two-month conflict with Iran escalates, cracks are appearing in the US-Israel alliance. While both nations initially targeted Tehran, their post-strike objectives diverge, with Washington seeking negotiation and Jerusalem aiming for regime change. This fundamental difference, exacerbated by past events and differing pressures, raises questions about their long-term alignment.

title: 'I hate to ... ': CSK captain Ruturaj Gaikwad's blunt verdict
content: Ruturaj Gaikwad expressed disappointment after Chennai Super Kings' eight-wicket loss to Gujarat Titans. Despite his unbeaten 74, CSK struggled to adapt to inconsistent bounce, falling short of a higher total. Gaikwad highlighted the difficulty in assessing the pitch and the impact of losing wickets at crucial junctures, preventing them from reaching a more competitive score.

title: Why vivid dreams feel stronger: Sleep experts decode restless nights
content: A restless night filled with vivid dreams isn't due to dreaming more, but rather failing to forget. Neuroscience suggests waking during REM sleep, when the brain's logic centers are offline, traps dream fragments in conscious memory. This interrupted forgetting, especially in early morning REM cycles, makes nights feel lived rather than slept.

title: Lights, camera...gone? The slow fade of India's OG photostudios
content: Neighbourhood photo studios, once bustling hubs, are now struggling as digital cameras and smartphones have made photography an everyday habit. Photographers lament the loss of business, with many now relying on outdoor events and regional film projects to survive. This shift reflects a societal change in how memories are captured and consumed.

title: Sanju Samson pulls off MS Dhoni-like stunner to dismiss Shubman Gill - WATCH
content: Sanju Samson showcased exceptional wicketkeeping skills, mirroring MS Dhoni's brilliance with a lightning-fast stumping of Shubman Gill. Earlier, Samson achieved a significant batting milestone, becoming the fastest Indian to score 5,000 IPL runs in terms of balls faced, solidifying his status as a consistent performer.

title: India's wheat outlook: Production stable despite challenges, says government
content: India's agriculture ministry anticipates stable wheat production for the 2025-26 crop year, despite localized weather disruptions. While an industry estimate suggests lower output, the ministry highlights increased acreage, early sowing, and improved seed varieties as factors compensating for adverse weather, ensuring resilient national production.

title: 'UCC rollout, triple talaq ban ... ': Amit Shah lists BJP's key promises at Bengal rally
content: Home Minister Amit Shah unveiled major BJP promises for West Bengal, including implementing Uniform Civil Code, banning triple talaq, and ending polygamy. He also pledged financial aid for women and unemployed youth, and a crackdown on cattle smuggling. Shah highlighted national security achievements and vowed to expel illegal infiltrators if the BJP wins.

title: Mali violence escalates: Defence minister Sadio Camara killed in 'terrorist attack'
content: Mali's security crisis deepened with the killing of Defence Minister Sadio Camara in an attack on his residence. Coordinated strikes by separatist fighters and Al-Qaeda-linked militants targeted Bamako and other cities, marking a significant escalation. The separatists confirmed an alliance with JNIM, complicating counterinsurgency efforts.

title: Fifty off 49: Why Ruturaj Gaikwad's knock hurt CSK more than it helped
content: Gaikwad remained unbeaten on 74 off 60 balls. A 74 not out can be valuable, or it can be the reason your team ends up 30 runs short. On Sunday at Chepauk, Ruturaj Gaikwad’s unbeaten 74 balls belonged firmly in the second category.

title: Watch: How Vance was evacuated amid gunfire panic at White House correspondents' dinner
content: Gunfire erupted outside a media gala attended by President Trump and Vice President Vance at the Washington Hilton, prompting a swift evacuation of officials. Secret Service agents apprehended the armed suspect, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, before he could reach the ballroom. No dignitaries were injured, though one officer was struck in his vest.

title: 'Ran towards ballroom': Volunteer recalls WH gunman assembled 'long weapon' in 'makeshift room'
content: A White House Correspondents' Dinner volunteer reported the suspected gunman assembled a "long weapon" in an unsecured area near a terrace entrance before the attack. The suspect then moved toward the ballroom, firing shots and causing panic among guests and staff. The gunman, identified as Cole Allen, was arrested after allegedly entering the Washington Hilton hotel.

title: BJP attacking Bengali culture, pride: Kejriwal; Modi won’t have his way in Bengal: Tejashwi
content: AAP convenor Arvind Kejriwal and RJD leader Tejaswi Yadav are campaigning in the State in support of Trinamool Congress even as Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has launched scathing attacks on Trinamool Congress government and Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Drug menace emerges as key plank in Amit Shah’s campaign for Punjab polls
content: Top sources in the BJP have told The Hindu that Mr. Shah will be visiting the State as often as once a month, through the year and to each district

title: For two women, a fight for justice in Bengal poll contest
content: Contesting from rival parties, BJP’s Ratna Debnath and CMI(M)’s Sabina Yasmin have turned personal loss into broader questions of safety, governance, and State accountability.

title: Experts seek data-led approach to tackle public health risks
content: The webinar discussed challenges from infectious diseases to NCDs, climate-linked risks, and the growing role of AI and policy in building resilient healthcare systems

title: Disqualify all seven defecting MPs, AAP says in petition to Rajya Sabha Chairman
content: Group led by Raghav Chadha claims that it fulfills required two-third strength making the merger with the BJP legal; however, the AAP has said that such a decision has to be taken by the party

title: Union government to resume talks on Ladakh on May 22
content: Amit Shah to visit Ladakh soon; the Ladakh-Centre talks hit a stalemate after violence erupted in Leh in September last year, which left four locals dead and over 80, including security personnel, injured

title: KWML awaits three ferries to extend Water Metro services to Kadamakudy
content: At present, the 20 ferries in operation across more than half a dozen routes are recording an average daily patronage of 7,500 commuters, rising to over 10,000 on weekends and public holidays

title: Provide fixed fund of at least ₹1 crore for academic chairs in universities: Prof. Baraguru Committee to government
content: While some academic chairs have a capital of ₹5 lakh, others have been given up to ₹5 crore. There is no uniformity in the amount of capital given to chairs, and this disparity should be eliminated, the committee proposed

title: Credai Chennai gets new team of leaders for 2026-28
content: Mehul H Doshi assumed office as the 10th President, along with the newly elected office bearers and executive committee members

title: Blaze at scrapyard exposes Kochi’s fire risks again
content: The District Disaster Management Authority has initiated steps to chalk out an action plan to prevent the recurrence of such incidents

title: Palacode, Veerapandi and Kulithalai frontrunners in voter turnout since 2011
content: They saw high turnout in every Assembly poll held in the State since the 2008 delimitation. This is due to several factors, including political mobilisation on communal lines and local-level loyalties

title: WRD restores key irrigation tank that remains major water source for Vellore Corporation
content: WRD officials said that the rejuvenation of the tank was done under the Rehabilitation and Restoration of Twin Lakes scheme at a cost of ₹19 crore

title: Residents complain of suffocation after open landfill site catches fire near Gudiyatham
content: At present, 25-30 metric tonnes of garbage, including domestic waste, are dumped at the landfill site every day

title: Advertorial tracing Ramesh Chennithala’s political odyssey raises eyebrows in UDF circles
content: Advertorial, which risked being perceived as a political hagiography, appeared close on the heels of the Kerala Pradesh Congress Committee president warning the party’s rank and file against jumping the gun and promoting their choices for Congress Legislative Party leadership post on television, social and print media

title: Suspected West Nile fever cases trigger health warning
content: The cases were reported from Elanji, Eroor in Thripunithura, Pallikkara in Kizhakkambalam, Ponekkara in Edappally, and Palluruthy

title: Haryana Congress manifesto for civic polls promises clean, well-equipped and corruption-free cities
content: Laying out a 15-point plan focused on sanitation, infrastructure, safety and governance, the party promised to transform cities into “Green Cities” with extensive green belts, beautified parks and public squares

title: 66% turnout for civic polls as people brave heat to vote in Gujarat
content: The elections are being held for 15 municipal corporations, including Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara and Rajkot, 84 municipalities, 34 district panchayats and 260 taluka panchayats, making it one of the largest electoral exercises in the State.

title: 66% turnout for civic polls as people brave heat to vote in Gujarat
content: Polling in municipal corporations comparatively lower than in other local bodies: SEC; AAP candidate among three reported dead on polling day

title: UAE-Iran differences and India’s plans to dilute language on Israel-Palestine derail joint statement at BRICS meet
content: Deeply polarised deputy FMs meeting in Delhi spell trouble for India’s BRICS Presidency; as the language of a joint statement eluded diplomats, India issued a ‘Chair’s Statement’ that reflected the subjects discussed

title: Govt. may establish labs in varsities to train students for space sector
content: The country’s private space ecosystem has expanded rapidly, with the number of startups increasing from a single digit in 2019 to more than 400 by early 2026

title: NGT issues notice to Centre over risks from hanging glaciers in central Himalaya
content: Taking suo motu cognisance of a report on hanging glacier risks, the tribunal said it indicated violations of the Environment (Protection) Act and raised concerns over compliance with environmental norms

title: ‘Kozhikode-Kannur stretch recorded most stone attacks on trains in past three years’
content: Measures are in place to improve surveillance at vulnerable locations, says an RPF officer; the involvement of suspects linked to drug cases is also being probed

title: Trinamool running a reign of terror in West Bengal, says  Rahul Gandhi
content: Condoling the death of a Congress worker in Asansol who the party has claimed was beaten up by ‘miscreants’ linked to the ruling Trinamool, the Leader of the Opposition in Lok Sabha said his party will not bow  before ‘this politics that stains India’s non-violent tradition’

title: Rahul Gandhi plans Great Nicobar visit, clearance awaited
content: The Union government is planning a ₹92,000-crore mega infrastructure project in Great Nicobar Island amid tribal communities’ concerns that they will be displaced and lose their rights over the forests there

title: As campaign reaches a crescendo, Modi reaches out to Matuas, Mamata camps at Bhabanipur
content: PM Modi tells Matua community not to fall for the ‘Trinamool’s lies on CAA’ and promises that ‘all refugees will receive permanent address in this country’; CM Mamata says her party has ‘scored a century in the first round’ of polls and that the ‘BJP is scared’

title: Raghu Rai | 1942-2026
content: Raghu Rai’s story is intricately linked with that of India. Without either, the story would be incomplete

title: TMC lodges complaint against BJP candidate over alleged misuse of national flag
content: TMC’s Kohinoor Mazumdar alleges BJP’s Papia Adhikari was seen wiping her face with a dupatta bearing the Indian tricolour, terms it a violation of the Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act

title: SARPA to link doctors, experts for real-time snakebite guidance
content: The Snake Awareness, Rescue and Protection App has played a crucial role in recent years by coordinating snake rescues, enabling users to report sightings, and providing details of trained responders and nearby healthcare facilities

title: Cheru Poorams keep Thrissur’s festive soul alive
content: From caparisoned elephants to resonant percussion, the processions turned the temple grounds into a living canvas of tradition

title: ‘Maintain consistency’: toppers offer tips to Civil Service aspirants
content: Meera K., a 2021 batch IAS officer, says the aim of the aspirants should not be just to clear an exam, but to become an efficient officer who contributes to society

title: Former BJD MP Manjulata Mandal, ex-MLA husband Muktikanta and others quit party
content: Addressing a meeting in Bhadrak on Sunday, Muktikanta Mandal alleged that despite dedicating years to strengthening the party, he was “unconstitutionally suspended” due to a conspiracy by vested interests

title: Mumbai-Pune Missing Link project to be inaugurated on May 1
content: The project will reduce travel time by half an hour; according to Maharashtra State Road Development Corporation officials, the road will bring relief to commuters from congestion at Khandala Ghat

title: ‘Civil service exam tests emotional fortitude rather than intellectual capability’
content: Benjo P. Jose, a 2024 batch IAS officer, emphasises importance of managing emotional dimension of the exam at the The Hindu Civil Service Gateway 2026

title: Trade deal with U.S. harming farmers’ interests, says AIKS leader
content: Farmers and tenant cultivators are facing an unprecedented crisis due to flawed policies of successive governments, says Ashok Dhawale

title: Third attempt: Defiant Trump shrugs off gunman threat
content: col Tomas Allen, 32, tried to storm into the banquet hall where the president was attending the annual white house correspondents association dinner . the gathering of media figures, social elites, and political heavies, including Trump’s cabinet associates, had just settled in for a pre-speech dinner when several gun shots were heard outside the hall . no shots were fired at the president himself or at the gathering .

title: Iran strikes caused more damage to US bases than disclosed, repairs may cost billions
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. title: DGCA tightens rules for foreign airlines operating in country
content: the DGCA can "revoke or suspend" a foreign airline's authorisation for lapses like not meeting safety standards . foreign airlines must set up an effective passenger grievance redressal mechanism . grounds for cancelling or revoking authorisation include ownership and control not vested with the designating country .

title: Brics consensus on Middle East elusive because of sharp differences, govt backs Palestine cause
content: the meeting of senior foreign ministers and special envoys could not produce a consensus document . there was sharp difference of positions among members who are party to the conflict, official sources say . a chair’s statement was issued which said the Members expressed deep concern on the recent conflict in the Middle East .

title: West Bengal polls: PM woos Matua community, vows to fast-track citizenship
content: prime minister addresses public meeting for west Bengal assembly elections . he reiterated his resolve to fast-track Indian citizenship for its members . in a message that emphasised his association with the sect, he posted a picture of his 2019 meeting with its matriarch .

title: Firing on Trump was inevitable, mood similar here: Maharashtra Cong MLA
content: Vijay Wadettiwar said the firing at the white house correspondents' association dinner was inevitable . he refrained from naming any neta or constitutional post . "this should not have happened to someone holding such a high offii," he said .

title: Two beamers by Tyagi, last-ball six by Shami: How LSG vs KKR went into Super Over
content: At TOI Sports Desk, reporters work around the clock to bring you comprehensive updates from the world of sports. Expect nuanced match reports, previews, and reviews, along with statistics-based technical analysis, the latest social media trends, and expert insights across cricket, football, tennis, badminton, hockey, motorsports, wrestling, boxing, shooting, athletics, and more. Read More

title: Row over Uttarakhand mantri son's Rajaji temple wedding
content: preparations for the wedding of cabinet minister Khajan Das's son were carried out inside the core zone of Rajaji Tiger Reserve (RTR) on Saturday, materials for a large setup - a pandal, a stage, coolers and generators - were taken into the reserve area . the forest department registered a case against the temple committee under provisions of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 .

title: Solar infra helps India meet record 256GW power demand
content: at the time of peak demand of 256.1 GW reached at 3.38pm, solar contributed nearly 57 GW, or about 22% of total generation . electricity generation from solar plants rose to about 81 GW around 12.30 pm, accounting for roughly one-third of the total generation of 242 GW .

title: Trump Event Shooting Highlights: Accused Shooter Likely Targeted Trump And His Top Officials
content: Shooting at Trump's event live updates: Donald Trump was rushed out of the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after suspected gunshots were heard at the event.

title: 10 Minutes Before Firing At Trump Event, Shooter Sent A Manifesto To Family
content: About ten minutes before Allen opened fire, he sent what has been described as an anti-Trump manifesto to members of his family.

title: Trump Event Shooter's Manifesto Spares FBI Chief Kash Patel From Hit List
content: In a manifesto sent to family members roughly ten minutes before he opened fire, Allen laid out exactly who he intended to target.

title: India, New Zealand To Sign "Once In A Generation" Free Trade Deal Today
content: Christopher Luxon said, "New Zealand's Free Trade Agreement with India will be signed on Monday night," while sharing a video outlining the expected benefits of the deal.

title: Trump Event Shooter Mocked Hotel Security In Manifesto, Said 'Won't Use Slugs'
content: Cole Allen wrote in the manifesto that he sensed "arrogance" in the security agents as he walked into the hotel with multiple weapons without being thoroughly checked.

title: Veggies, Not Jhalmuri: Mamata Banerjee's Casual Response To PM Modi's Snack Break
content: The 71-year-old Chief Minister was seen going for the healthier options, even throwing in a bunch of fruits.

title: As Kash Patel's Past Catches Up, Report Hints At Trump Axe
content: Patel has found himself at the centre of an unwanted spotlight this week after reports emerged about two arrests during his younger years.

title: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi Meets Asim Munir After Landing In Islamabad
content: There was no official word from Pakistan about the meeting which apparently focused on the ongoing peace efforts by Islamabad to arrange the second round of talks between the US and Iran.

title: Arvind Kejriwal Asks Bengal's Deleted Voters To Back Trinamool, Defeat BJP
content: Arvind Kejriwal urged those whose names were allegedly deleted from the voters' list to support the Trinamool Congress.

title: Amid Iran's Attacks, UAE Called For Help. Israel Responded With Iron Dome
content: Jerusalem also sent troops to operate the air defence system that is famously used to intercept short-range rockets, missiles, and drones.

title: Bangladeshi Hindu Temple Caretaker's Body Found Hanging From Tree
content: Two unidentified men took Nayan with them late on the evening of April 19, and three days later, his body was found hanging from a tree in a hilly area on the outskirts of the village.

title: 'BJP Has Already Won 110 Seats In 1st Phase Of Bengal Polls': Amit Shah
content: Amit Shah said if the BJP is voted to power, it would identify "infiltrators hiding in the state" and take necessary action against them.

title: Delhi Cop Fires At Delivery Agent, Bullet Pierces And Kills Him, Hits Another
content: Both the injured men were immediately rushed to a local hospital, where Pandav was pronounced dead, while Krishna is currently undergoing treatment.

title: Donald Trump Pushes 'Ballroom' Agenda With 'Won't Have Happened' Post On Shooting
content: Donald Trump's plan to construct a 90,000 square-foot structure on the former site of the White House East Wing for $400 million has hit a legal hurdle.
